meta.title: Safe Work Procedure — Manual Print-Head Changeover, Press 4
meta.subtitle: Plant 2, Maharashtra — planned stop · frontline-operator literacy level
meta.author: HSE Department
meta.classification: Internal — competent-person review required before issue
meta.deid_notice: All personnel are referred to by role label. No identifiers appear in the responsibilities or approval blocks; the approval block carries role + signature placeholder only.

sections.text: This safe work procedure (SWP) covers the manual changeover of the print-head on Press 4 during a planned stop. It is grounded in ISO 45001 clause 8.1 (operational control) and integrates with the existing job-safety analysis JSA-2026-014 (its hazards and rated controls are ingested and cross-referenced, not re-scored). It is written to a frontline-operator literacy level: short imperative steps, one action per step.

items: IN scope: mechanical changeover of the print-head on Press 4 during a planned, de-energised stop.
OUT of scope: electrical fault repair (requires a competent electrician under a separate permit); print-head calibration beyond the function-test.
Hazard source: ingested from JSA-2026-014 (cross-referenced by id) — this procedure does not re-score risk.
Authored to ISO 45001 8.1 operational control; reading level = frontline operator (KB-SNIP-AUDIENCE).

rows: Authorise the planned stop; issue + close the isolation permit; verify zero energy.
Lock-out/tag-out authorised-person certification; PTW issuer training.
Execute the changeover steps; apply personal lock; perform the function-test.
Press 4 operator competency; LOTO awareness; SWP induction.
Verify the procedure is followed; sign the completion of the changeover.
Front-line supervisor competency; this SWP reviewed.
Line de-energised + locked out (Engineering/Admin isolation) BEFORE work begins; zero-energy confirmation [check].
Fixed guard interlock proven open (Engineering); two-hand technique; cut-resistant gloves [PPE].
Allow cool-down hold-point [check]; cut-resistant gloves + face shield [PPE].

sections.text: Hierarchy of controls — the control plan

rows.control: De-energise & lock out Press 4 before any work (isolation regime)
Fixed guard interlock proven open before guard removal
Cool-down hold-point before touching the print-head

rows: Stop Press 4 and tell the Authorised Person you are ready to isolate.
Planned stop only — do not start if the press is mid-run.
Isolate Press 4. Apply your personal lock. Confirm zero energy.
LOTO: line locked out [Engineering]; confirm zero-energy reading [check/hold-point] — do NOT proceed until confirmed.
Guard interlock proven open [Engineering]; wear cut-resistant gloves [PPE]; use two hands [technique].
Wait for the print-head to cool, then unclip it.
Cool-down hold-point — confirm cool to touch [check]; face shield + gloves [PPE].
Fit the replacement print-head and clip it home.
Refit the guard. Confirm the interlock re-engages.
Remove your personal lock. The Authorised Person closes the permit.
Only the lock owner removes their lock [Admin].
Function-test the press at low speed. Stop if anything is abnormal.
Stop condition: any abnormal noise/movement → re-isolate [hold-point].

items: PPE (secondary to the step-embedded engineering controls): cut-resistant gloves; face shield.
Equipment: personal lock + tag (LOTO); replacement print-head; torque tool.
Permit: isolation/LOTO permit issued by the Authorised Person.

sections.text: STOP conditions — re-isolate Press 4 immediately and call the Supervisor if: zero energy cannot be confirmed; the guard interlock does not prove open/closed; the function-test shows abnormal noise or movement.

items: Hold-point failure (zero-energy or interlock not confirmed): do NOT proceed; re-isolate and escalate to the Authorised Person.
First aid: nearest first-aider via the plant emergency number; report any contact injury under the incident process.
Do not improvise an electrical repair — raise a separate permit for a competent electrician (out of scope).
Ingested hazard analysis: JSA-2026-014 (cross-referenced by id — method ↔ JSA).
India / Maharashtra: the documented-procedure duty under the resolved state Factory Rules via KB-REG-IN-STATEFORMS (state confirmed; no SOP form number — an SOP is an internal MS document).

rows: Review on change (MoC-triggered) or at minimum annually [ASSUMPTION — user gave no cycle; competent-person reviewer to confirm]
